message:
feat(vdbe,compat): add streaming row callback API to avoid buffering all result rows in memory

Introduce a per-row callback path through the VDBE engine so callers
can process rows as they arrive instead of collecting them all into a
Vec<Row> first. This eliminates the intermediate allocation for large
result sets.

**VDBE engine (fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs):**
New ResultRowCallback type alias and
execute_with_borrowed_bindings_and_row_handler method. Refactors
execute_with_borrowed_bindings into an _internal variant accepting an
optional per-row callback. When handler is provided,
execute_result_row_hot invokes it immediately instead of pushing rows
into self.results. Unit test verifies rows stream through callback
while engine buffer stays empty.

**Core connection (fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs):**
Wire new engine capability into query_with_params_for_each. Parse first,
use prepared-statement reuse only if supported. Non-reusable statements
(e.g. EXPLAIN) executed via streaming path through
execute_statement_after_background_status.

**Compat layer (fsqlite/src/compat/connection.rs, transaction.rs):**
query_map_collect switches from query_with_params (buffer-then-map) to
query_with_params_for_each (stream-and-map), avoiding intermediate
Vec<Row> allocation. Tests added for side-effect-only callbacks and
EXPLAIN statements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
